hero.headline: MUJ Online MBA in Supply Chain Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved Super/Dual specialization. 12 spec subjects in Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #58.

sections.tldr: MUJ Online MBA in Supply Chain Management covers supply chain management, outsourcing, food supply chain management, inventory management, global logistics, category management in purchasing, purchasing and contracting, and supply chain cost management for professionals targeting supply chain director or COO track roles. NAAC A+, NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000 upfront (15% off) or Rs 1,80,000. Super/Dual spec — pair with Operations or International Business. Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (70% off).

about.body:
Supply Chain Management is the MBA specialization for professionals who manage procurement, sourcing, logistics, and end-to-end supply networks: procurement managers, logistics heads, warehouse operations executives, and supply chain analysts who want to move into Supply Chain Director, VP Procurement, or COO roles. The MUJ Online MBA in Supply Chain Management covers the full supply chain discipline: end-to-end supply chain design, outsourcing strategy, food supply chain management, inventory optimisation, global logistics, category management, purchasing and contracting, and supply chain cost management.

MUJ's Super/Dual specialization creates powerful supply chain credential combinations. Pairing Supply Chain Management with Operations Management creates an end-to-end manufacturing and logistics profile for plant and operations director roles. Pairing with International Business creates a global trade and logistics credential for export-import, 3PL, and MNC supply chain roles. Both combinations are sought by manufacturing companies, logistics firms, and retailers with complex supply networks.

The Coursera Plus bundle at Rs 3,999 gives Supply Chain students access to supply chain analytics, procurement management, and logistics courses from Rutgers University and Coursera's supply chain specialization partners — practical certifications that complement the MUJ curriculum.

Manipal University Jaipur Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #58 University ranking for 2025. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from MUJ. Full programme overview at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Supply chain strategy, competitive advantage through procurement, and aligning supply chain investment with business goals.

sem3Subjects.name: Term Paper
sem3Subjects.description: Independent research paper applying supply chain management theory to a real-world procurement or logistics challenge.

sem3Subjects.name: Supply Chain Management
sem3Subjects.description: Supply chain network design, demand planning, supplier relationship management, and supply chain performance measurement.

sem3Subjects.name: Outsourcing
sem3Subjects.description: Outsourcing strategy, make-or-buy decision frameworks, vendor selection, SLA design, and managing outsourced supply chain functions.

sem3Subjects.name: Food Supply Chain Management
sem3Subjects.description: Cold chain management, food safety standards, perishable logistics, and supply chain compliance for FMCG and food companies.

sem3Subjects.name: Inventory Management
sem3Subjects.description: EOQ and safety stock models, ABC analysis, demand forecasting, warehouse slotting, and inventory carrying cost optimisation.

sem4Subjects.name: International Business Management
sem4Subjects.description: Global supply chain strategy, cross-border sourcing, import duty optimisation, and managing multinational procurement networks.

sem4Subjects.name: Project
sem4Subjects.description: Major dissertation applying supply chain management learning to an original applied procurement or logistics study.

sem4Subjects.name: Global Logistics and Supply Chain Management
sem4Subjects.description: International freight management, incoterms, customs clearance, 3PL and 4PL models, and global distribution network design.

sem4Subjects.name: Category Management in Purchasing
sem4Subjects.description: Spend category analysis, strategic sourcing, supplier market analysis, and category strategy development for procurement.

sem4Subjects.name: Purchasing and Contracting for Projects
sem4Subjects.description: Procurement planning for capital projects, contract type selection, tendering process management, and supplier performance governance.

sem4Subjects.name: Supply Chain Cost Management
sem4Subjects.description: Total cost of ownership analysis, should-cost modelling, supply chain cost driver identification, and cost reduction programme management.

syllabus.note: Semester 1 and 2 subjects are shared across all MUJ Online MBA specializations. Full programme structure at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

whoHires.body:
Supply Chain Management MBA graduates from Manipal University Jaipur Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** FMCG and food companies, Manufacturing, E-commerce logistics, Retail, 3PL and freight forwarding

**Entry-level roles:** Supply Chain Analyst, Procurement Executive, Logistics Coordinator, Inventory Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Supply Chain Manager, Procurement Manager, Category Manager, Logistics Manager, Demand Planning Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Director of Supply Chain, VP Procurement, Head of Logistics, COO (supply chain-led companies)

**Top hiring organisations:** HUL, ITC, Nestle India, Tata Consumer Products, Amazon India Operations, Flipkart Supply Chain, Delhivery, Maersk India, DHL, Reliance Retail

skills.body:
The Supply Chain Management specialization builds skills that FMCG, manufacturing, and e-commerce employers test at supply chain manager and director levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Demand Planning: statistical forecasting methods, S&OP process design, demand sensing, and forecast accuracy measurement
- Procurement: strategic sourcing methodology, should-cost analysis, RFQ and tender management, and supplier performance KPI frameworks
- Inventory Optimisation: safety stock calculation, ABC-XYZ segmentation, cycle counting methodology, and inventory reduction programme design
- Global Logistics: freight mode selection analysis, 3PL contract management, customs documentation, and landed cost calculation

**Applied skills:**
- Category Management: spend analysis, category strategy development, supplier base rationalisation, and total cost of ownership optimisation
- Supply Chain Cost Management: cost driver mapping, supply chain benchmarking, and negotiation preparation using should-cost models

**Soft skills:** Presenting supply chain performance and cost reduction programmes to CFOs, managing cross-functional S&OP meetings, negotiating supplier contracts at head-of-category level, and communicating supply chain risk to non-operational leadership.

comparisons.body:
Comparing MUJ Online MBA Supply Chain Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Supply Chain Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Supply Chain covers logistics and procurement in depth. MUJ Supply Chain has food supply chain management and supply chain cost management as differentiated subjects — relevant for FMCG and retail supply chain roles. MUJ's Rs 1,53,000 upfront fee is 32% lower. Amity NIRF #22 carries stronger weight at MNC FMCG companies.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences:** NMIMS is NAAC A++ and NIRF Management #24. NMIMS covers supply chain alongside operations and data sciences. MUJ Supply Chain gives dedicated 12 spec subjects entirely focused on procurement, logistics, and supply chain strategy. For pure supply chain professionals, MUJ's dedicated spec provides more coverage depth. NMIMS brand is overall stronger.

**vs LPU Online MBA Supply Chain Management:** LPU is NAAC A++ with supply chain offering. LPU covers logistics and procurement. MUJ's Super/Dual spec (pairing Supply Chain with Operations or International Business) and Coursera Plus access are advantages. Manipal brand recognition at pan-India FMCG and logistics employers is a differentiator.

fees.body:
The total program fee for the Manipal University Jaipur Online MBA in Supply Chain Management can be paid through 2 options:

**Option 1 — Semester-wise payment**: Rs 45,000 per semester, Rs 1,80,000 total across 4 semesters
**Option 2 — Full upfront payment (discounted)**: Rs 1,53,000 one-time (Rs 1,80,000 standard less 15 percent fee concession)

A one-time registration fee of Rs 500 applies at enrolment. MUJ does not charge separate exam fees per attempt, project evaluation fees, or admission processing fees beyond this.

For working professionals, MUJ offers a 24-month zero-cost EMI plan at approximately Rs 7,500 per month on the standard fee (Rs 6,375 per month on the discounted fee) through its NBFC lending partners.

**Scholarships available** (non-stackable — pick one based on eligibility):
- **Divyaang** (persons with disabilities): 20% discount with Divyaang certificate
- **Defence personnel**: 20% discount with Service/IC number
- **Manipal University Jaipur alumni** (UG or PG): 20% discount
- **Government employees**: 10% discount with Govt ID
- **Merit scholarship**: 10% discount for 80%+ marks in 10+2 or bachelor's

**Coursera Plus bundled access** (MUJ differentiator): Enrolled MUJ students get Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (standard Rs 13,999 — a 70 percent discount). This includes 10,000+ courses from University of Virginia, University of Michigan, Johns Hopkins, University of California Irvine, and other top global universities.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees and scholarships with Manipal University Jaipur each quarter. Please reconfirm current offers and eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Manipal University Jaipur degree certificate carries the Manipal University Jaipur name, the MBA designation, and identifies the online learning mode per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The Supply Chain Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode identification on the certificate is standard for UGC-DEB approved programmes and equivalent in recognition to regular MBA degrees for employment, promotions, and further studies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from MUJ Online MBA Supply Chain Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Pradeep Singh
items.liked: Category management and supply chain cost management subjects gave me strategic procurement tools I had been missing in 9 years of sourcing experience
items.disliked: Digital supply chain and supply chain analytics content was minimal — increasingly important for senior supply chain roles at tech-forward companies
items.body: I head procurement for a mid-size FMCG company. The category strategy, should-cost modelling, and supplier performance management content was the best structured learning I had found outside of consulting firm training. Paired Supply Chain with Operations for an end-to-end operations and procurement credential. The Manipal brand was recognised immediately at HUL and ITC supply chain interviews. Got promoted to Head of Category Management six months after completing the programme.

items.name: Leena Thomas
items.liked: Food supply chain management was uniquely relevant — I work for a food processing company and the cold chain and food safety content applied directly
items.disliked: Live session attendance tracking was inconsistent and one semester's attendance record was not properly updated in the system
items.body: I manage supply chain for a food processing firm. The food supply chain, inventory management, and global logistics content was directly applicable. Pairing with International Business added the export documentation and trade finance coverage I needed for our export expansion. The Manipal NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 were verified by our parent company's HR team in Singapore without issues. Career support was not useful but I was not joining for placement.

items.name: Nitin Sharma
items.liked: Inventory management and demand planning frameworks were practically taught and I applied the safety stock calculations immediately
items.disliked: Placement support was weak for supply chain roles. Career portal had no connections with 3PL companies, FMCG supply chain teams, or logistics firms
items.body: The content across the 12 spec subjects was solid. Inventory, procurement, and global logistics subjects were thorough. My frustration was placement — the online career services team had zero relationships with supply chain hiring managers. Every interview I got was through direct applications on LinkedIn. The Manipal credential helped with shortlisting. Beyond that, the programme offers no structured placement assistance for supply chain roles.

faqs.question: Can I choose dual specialization with the MUJ Online MBA in Supply Chain Management?
faqs.answer: Yes. MUJ's Super/Dual model lets you pair Supply Chain Management with a second specialization at no extra cost. Strong combinations: Supply Chain plus Operations Management (for end-to-end manufacturing and logistics director roles), Supply Chain plus International Business (for global trade and export logistics careers), or Supply Chain plus Analytics and Data Science (for data-driven supply chain and demand planning roles). Both specs appear on your marksheet.

faqs.question: Is Coursera Plus access included or charged separately?
faqs.answer: Coursera Plus is available to MUJ students at Rs 3,999 per year (standard Rs 13,999 — 70% off). For Supply Chain students, Coursera Plus covers the Rutgers supply chain management specialization, logistics and transportation courses, and procurement analytics courses. The APICS CSCP or CPIM exam prep materials are also available through Coursera. At Rs 3,999, this supplements the MUJ curriculum at very low cost.

faqs.question: What scholarships are available for the MUJ Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Five non-stackable scholarship categories: 20% for Divyaang individuals, 20% for Defence personnel, 20% for MUJ alumni, 10% for Government employees, and 10% merit scholarship for 80%+ marks in 10+2 or bachelor's. Plus, paying the full fee upfront gets a 15% fee concession — reducing the total from Rs 1,80,000 to Rs 1,53,000.

faqs.question: What salary can Supply Chain MBA graduates from MUJ expect?
faqs.answer: Supply chain managers and procurement managers with 4-7 years of experience and this MBA earn Rs 12-22 lakh at FMCG, manufacturing, and logistics companies. Category heads and supply chain directors earn Rs 20-38 lakh. COO roles at supply chain-intensive mid-size companies pay Rs 28-55 lakh. Outcomes depend on sector (FMCG vs pharma vs e-commerce), company scale, and whether the candidate supplements with APICS CSCP or CPIM certification.

faqs.question: Is this MBA relevant for professionals in the e-commerce logistics sector?
faqs.answer: Yes. The global logistics, inventory management, and supply chain network design subjects are directly applicable to fulfilment, last-mile, and reverse logistics roles at e-commerce companies. The food supply chain subject is also increasingly relevant for quick-commerce platforms managing perishable inventory. Pairing Supply Chain with Analytics and Data Science creates a demand forecasting and fulfilment analytics profile that e-commerce logistics employers actively recruit for.

chooseIf: You are a supply chain, procurement, or logistics professional targeting supply chain director or VP Procurement roles that require a management degree.
You want 12 spec subjects covering supply chain strategy, category management, global logistics, and cost management at a NAAC A+ university under Rs 1.8 lakh.
You can pair Supply Chain with Operations or International Business for an end-to-end operations or global trade credential using MUJ's Super/Dual spec.
consider: You need the strongest supply chain brand for MNC FMCG supply chain leadership — Amity NIRF #22 or NMIMS carry stronger recognition at Tier 1 FMCG and consulting firms.

You are targeting APICS CSCP or CPIM certification specifically — complete those certifications independently as they carry direct practitioner recognition for procurement and logistics specialist roles.
You need active placement support for supply chain roles — MUJ online career services do not maintain connections with FMCG, logistics, or e-commerce supply chain teams.
